target.title: fix(ui): scope chat session picker to active agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Control UI chat session picker can show sessions from other configured agents because picker search/load-more calls are not scoped by active agent and the popover renders raw `sessions.list` rows.
- Why it matters: multi-agent users can see and accidentally switch into unrelated agent/service sessions from the chat picker.
- What changed: picker requests now include the active `agentId`, and the popover renders rows through the same filtered session-option projection used by the grouped selector.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the full Sessions page and gateway `sessions.list` behavior remain unchanged.

AI-assisted: Yes. I understand the code path and verified the targeted behavior locally.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85963
- Supersedes #85964 after rebasing onto current `main`
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: chat session picker pagination/search built `sessions.list` params without the active `agentId`, then rendered `result.sessions` directly instead of using the existing filtered session option groups.
- Missing detection / guardrail: picker tests covered search/pagination mechanics but not cross-agent/cron/subagent filtering in the popover.
- Contributing context (if known): the grouped selector already had active-agent filtering, but the newer popover path bypassed it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when the picker result includes another agent, cron session, and spawned subagent, only active-agent chat sessions render in the popover.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the Control UI projection/render path, so a focused DOM render test covers the visible regression without a browser E2E fixture.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): grouped selector filtering tests in `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` covered the helper but not this popover path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The chat session picker now shows active-agent chat sessions by default instead of leaking unrelated agent/service rows into the popover.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[open chat picker] -> [sessions.list without agentId] -> [raw rows rendered] -> [cross-agent rows visible]

After:
[open chat picker] -> [sessions.list with active agentId] -> [filtered option rows rendered] -> [active-agent sessions visible]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: UI session-list access is narrowed for this picker by passing `agentId` and filtering display rows; broader session management surfaces are unchanged.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local pnpm workspace and installed local OpenClaw Control UI
- Model/provider: N/A for test execution
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI chat
- Relevant config (redacted): multiple configured agents with agent-scoped session keys

### Steps

1. Open the Control UI chat view with multiple configured agents and sessions.
2. Open the chat session picker.
3. Search/load more from the picker.

### Expected

- Picker requests are scoped to the active agent.
- Popover renders only sessions allowed by the grouped session selector filtering.

### Actual

- Before this PR, picker requests were unscoped and the popover rendered raw `sessions.list` rows.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Redacted runtime diagnostic output
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 11.10s

pnpm check:test-types
completed successfully

pnpm build
completed successfully
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: the Control UI chat session picker can leak unrelated configured-agent/service rows when it requests sessions without the active agent scope and renders raw response rows.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw app, Control UI served from the installed app on `127.0.0.1:18789`, with multiple configured agents present. Private session labels, keys, and structure are intentionally redacted from the proof below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: refreshed the installed Control UI cache, inspected the served HTML/JS/SW, and queried the live gateway with an explicit active-agent filter. No screenshots are attached because the visible picker contains private session names.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:18789/ | rg -n 'index-BtIuF4zW\.js\?v=local-session-picker-fix|local-session-picker-fix'
198:    <script type="module" crossorigin src="./assets/index-BtIuF4zW.js?v=local-session-picker-fix"></script>

$ curl -sS 'http://127.0.0.1:18789/assets/index-BtIuF4zW.js?v=local-session-picker-fix' \
  | rg -o 'agentId:EW\(e,e\.sessionKey\)|i=AW\(e,e\.sessionKey,r\)\.flatMap|local-session-picker-fix'
agentId:EW(e,e.sessionKey)
i=AW(e,e.sessionKey,r).flatMap
local-session-picker-fix

$ curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:18789/sw.js \
  | rg -n 'local-session-picker-fix|cacheName: CACHE_NAME|caches\.match'
5:const EMBEDDED_CACHE_VERSION = "2026.5.22-a374c3a5bfd5-local-session-picker-fix";
64:      caches.match(event.request, { cacheName: CACHE_NAME }).then(
86:        .catch(() => caches.match(event.request)),

$ openclaw gateway call sessions.list --json --params '{"agentId":"main","limit":50}' \
  | jq '{agentScopedCount:(.sessions|length), containsForeignAgents:([.sessions[] | select((.agentId // "main") != "main")] | length > 0), visibleRowsRedacted:[.sessions[:5][] | {key:"<redacted>", label:"<redacted>", agentId:(.agentId // "main"), kind:(.kind // null)}]}'
{
  "agentScopedCount": 22,
  "containsForeignAgents": false,
  "visibleRowsRedacted": [
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "group" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the served picker bundle now sends the active `agentId`, projects picker rows through the existing filtered option groups, and the live gateway diagnostic for the active agent reports `containsForeignAgents: false` with session keys/labels redacted.
- What was not tested: public screenshot/recording of the picker, because that would reveal private session names and structure. The runtime diagnostic above is the privacy-preserving proof artifact.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: picker search params include `agentId`; popover filters out other-agent, cron, and spawned subagent rows; targeted UI tests pass; test typecheck passes; full build passes; installed local dist contains the scoped picker request and filtered-row projection.
- Edge cases checked: active session remains visible through existing `resolveSessionOptionGroups` behavior; pagination/search test expectations were updated for the scoped request.
- What you did **not** verify: public browser screenshots in a running Control UI instance, because they would expose private session names and structure.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the picker count can reflect the filtered visible rows while `totalCount` still comes from the server response.
  - Mitigation: server request is now scoped by `agentId`, and existing filtered option logic is reused for display consistency.

target.title: refactor: internalize OpenClaw agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- rename the former Pi agent runtime/package surfaces to OpenClaw-owned agent runtime names
- internalize the runtime packages into core and wire the embedded OpenClaw runtime through the current plugin/runtime contracts
- remove copied session-export HTML assets, package-manager install/update leftovers, runtime `.pi` auth discovery, and stale internal Pi naming
- split modern agent runtime id helpers from deprecated Pi runtime-id compatibility; keep compatibility in explicit doctor/session/env/plugin-boundary helpers
- keep deprecated plugin SDK compatibility only at named boundaries, including `resolveLegacyAgentAccessToken`
- add the Pi third-party notice for incorporated code

## Verification

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw no longer has live core/runtime paths depending on Pi package names or `.pi` credential discovery; remaining Pi runtime-id compatibility is isolated in deprecated compat helpers for shipped config/session/env values and plugin SDK boundary compatibility.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout plus Linux Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/aliases.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/backend.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/runtime-plugin.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>`; `git diff --check`; `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ... corepack pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: targeted Vitest passed 13 files / 181 tests; local tsgo passed; targeted oxlint passed; `git diff --check` passed; autoreview clean; Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ksafhp87h8heqjsjwb7z9wgk` passed `pnpm check:changed` in Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26333664224.
Observed result after fix: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runtime.ts` is gone; modern runtime-id helpers live in `src/agents/agent-runtime-id.ts`; deprecated Pi alias handling is quarantined in `src/agents/deprecated-agent-runtime-compat.ts`; plugin SDK `resolveEmbeddedAgentRuntime` remains exported.
What was not tested: full Vitest suite and live provider usage calls.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate the value as a string array, preserving explicit `[]` as an empty allowlist.
- Forward the allowlist through `SpawnSubagentParams` into the child `agent` Gateway request so the existing embedded-runner allowlist and tool construction path apply.
- Reject `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` because ACP does not use the embedded-runner tool construction path.
- Reject `toolsAllow` for CLI-backed native runs because CLI harnesses cannot enforce OpenClaw embedded-runner tool allowlists.
- Document the user-facing option and add an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Native persistent subagent child sessions that store `runtimeToolsAllow` inherit that allowlist on a later embedded run when the caller does not pass an explicit `toolsAllow`.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, current PR head `3f35c57d4d84b99c5f41ae2d95f5d3a487a0cc96`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and the repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the assertion over the mock request log:

```json
{
  "head": "3f35c57d4d84b99c5f41ae2d95f5d3a487a0cc96",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process; the persistent-session writer path is covered by the focused tests in this PR, while this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

### Earlier one-shot allowlist proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can now pass a narrow native subagent tool allowlist with `toolsAllow`; the value is exposed in the live tool schema and forwarded through the child Gateway `agent` path into the existing native embedded-runner allowlist filtering.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout from branch `sessions-spawn-tools-allow-pr2`.
- PR head tested: `09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebuilt dist with `pnpm build`, then used an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, loopback token-auth Gateway, temp workspace, temp mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server, and no live remote model/provider call. The same child Gateway `agent` path that `sessions_spawn` forwards into was run three times: without `toolsAllow`, with `toolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, and with `toolsAllow: []`.
- Evidence after fix: the mock OpenAI server captured the actual `tools` array sent by the embedded runner. Baseline sent 21 tools; the allowlisted run sent only `exec` and `read`; the explicit empty allowlist run sent 0 tools. Current-head focused tests also prove CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch for both `["read"]` and `[]`, and explicit empty embedded `toolsAllow` uses the minimal prompt path without the skills catalog.

```json
{
  "head": "09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385",
  "gatewayPort": 41167,
  "mockOpenAiBaseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:41168/v1",
  "baseline": {
    "label": "baseline",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-baseline-6262660c-a744-4330-972c-56051148dc92",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 2296,
    "toolCount": 21,
    "toolNames": [
      "edit",
      "exec",
      "image",
      "image_generate",
      "memory_get",
      "memory_search",
      "music_generate",
      "process",
      "read",
      "session_status",
      "sessions_history",
      "sessions_list",
      "sessions_send",
      "sessions_spawn",
      "sessions_yield",
      "subagents",
      "update_plan",
      "video_generate",
      "web_fetch",
      "web_search",
      "write"
    ]
  },
  "allowReadExec": {
    "label": "allow-read-exec",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-allow-read-exec-42d95c53-529a-460e-ba17-bab5ff57143b",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 440,
    "toolCount": 2,
    "toolNames": [
      "exec",
      "read"
    ]
  },
  "emptyAllow": {
    "label": "empty-allow",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-empty-allow-769c291b-1f33-4da9-afb3-644c0a1f3474",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 356,
    "toolCount": 0,
    "toolNames": []
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: final-head embedded native subagent runs enforce `toolsAllow` before the request reaches the model transport, and explicit `[]` disables tools instead of falling back to unrestricted defaults. CLI-backed native runs fail closed instead of silently ignoring the allowlist.
- Before evidence: without this PR, `sessions_spawn` had no `toolsAllow` schema entry and the spawned child Gateway `agent` request could not carry a per-spawn allowlist.
- What was not tested: a live remote model-backed spawned child run. The proof intentionally uses a mock OpenAI server to avoid real provider calls while still exercising the Gateway and embedded-runner path.

## Supplemental local timing note
The proof run also recorded local mock wall time: baseline `2296ms`, allowlisted `440ms`, empty allowlist `356ms`. This is included only as a local mock timing signal from one isolated run, not as a formal benchmark or guaranteed provider/model speedup. The primary value of this PR is correctness/control-plane narrowing; any latency win depends on tool set, runtime config, and provider behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)
N/A. This is additive feature plumbing over an existing embedded-runner allowlist mechanism.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects ACP runtime usage, forwards through Gateway `agent`, and reaches embedded runs started by the command runner.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: this PR connects an existing embedded-runner option to `sessions_spawn`; focused tests cover the schema, validation, and forwarding contract without requiring live model behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow the spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when the field is omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects the option with a clear input error.

## Diagram (if applicable)
N/A.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. ACP rejects the option because it does not use this embedded tool construction path.

## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS 26.2
- Runtime/container: Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local source checkout
- Model/provider: mock OpenAI `/v1/responses`; no live remote model call
- Integration/channel: local loopback Gateway `agent` RPC and `agent.wait`

- `pnpm build` passed before the current-head Gateway/mock proof.
- Final-head Gateway/mock proof above passed on `09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385`.

### Expected
- Omitted `toolsAllow`: default native child tool set is unchanged.
- `toolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`: the child model request only sees `read` and `exec` tools.
- `toolsAllow: []`: the child model request sees no tools.

### Actual
- Omitted `toolsAllow`: 21 tools captured by mock OpenAI.
- `toolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`: 2 tools captured by mock OpenAI, exactly `exec` and `read`.
- `toolsAllow: []`: 0 tools captured by mock OpenAI.

## Evidence
- [x] Gateway-backed terminal capture
- [x] Mock transport request capture
- [x] Focused tests, typecheck, lint, whitespace check

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, embedded command runner propagation, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist minimal prompt behavior, formatter/typecheck/lint/whitespace checks, final-head Gateway behavior proof.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.

## Review Conversations

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(imessage): detect and recover anchorless watch payloads before routing
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix iMessage group link-preview watch payloads that lose their conversation anchor (`chat_id=0`, empty `chat_guid` / `chat_identifier`, `is_group=false`) before they can be classified as sender DMs.
- Move anchorless recovery/fail-closed drop ahead of live inbound debounce classification so `coalesceSameSenderDms` cannot merge malformed group payloads under the raw `chat:0` DM bucket.
- Keep the existing recovery path for catchup/direct handling and add monitor-level regression coverage for the live coalescing path.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: iMessage group link-preview watch payloads can arrive without usable conversation anchor fields. Before this fix, live notifications entered the inbound debouncer first, so with `coalesceSameSenderDms=true` multiple anchorless group payloads from the same sender could be merged under `imessage:<account>:dm:chat:0:<sender>` before recovery. The merged turn was then repaired using only the first GUID and could route content from another group under the first recovered group.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0, OpenClaw source checkout at `e22876fd1befdbda3183cc33fb5162459791a89e`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483/node_modules/.bin/vitest run \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor.watch-subscribe-retry.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor.gating.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts

pnpm -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 check:test-types

env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483/node_modules/.bin/tsx \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84503-anchorless-proof.mts

OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19046 \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84503-final-runtime/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84503-final-runtime/state/openclaw.json \
node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway

curl -sS -I http://127.0.0.1:19046/
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       51 passed (51)
Duration    9.29s
```

```text
$ pnpm -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 check:test-types
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

```text
PASS unrecoverable anchorless payload is dropped fail-closed
BEFORE fix: raw payloads share imessage:default:dm:chat:0:+15550001111; merged GUIDs=["GUID-GROUP-A","GUID-GROUP-B"] route=chat_id:101
AFTER fix: payloads repair before debounce; keys=imessage:default:chat:101:+15550001111 / imessage:default:chat:202:+15550001111; routes=chat_id:101,chat_id:202
PASS coalescing risk closed: cross-chat anchorless payloads no longer enter one raw DM bucket
Overall: PASS
```

```text
2026-05-24T15:58:29.888+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 9.6s)
2026-05-24T15:58:30.678+08:00 [gateway] ready
```

```text
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
```

**Observed result after fix**: Anchorless live payloads are repaired before entering the inbound debouncer. Recovered group payloads have `is_group=true` and distinct group chat IDs before debounce classification, so `coalesceSameSenderDms` no longer places different malformed groups into the same raw `chat:0` DM bucket. Single anchorless payload recovery and unrecoverable fail-closed drops still work.

**What was not tested**: A real BlueBubbles/iMessage live watch feed was not connected in this Linux environment. The gateway startup used `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, so it proves the patched OpenClaw runtime starts and serves HTTP, but it does not exercise a live macOS Messages database or live BlueBubbles transport. target.title: fix(agents): log warnings instead of swallowing subagent errors
target.body:
## Problem

Two catch blocks in the subagent registry silently discard errors, leaving no diagnostic trail when hook emission or disk restore fails:

1. **`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`** (`subagent-registry-completion.ts:116`): `catch { return false; }` discards hook-runner errors when emitting the `subagent_ended` lifecycle hook.
2. **`restoreSubagentRunsOnce`** (`subagent-registry.ts:645`): `catch { // ignore restore failures }` discards errors when restoring subagent runs from disk on cold start.

When either path fails (hook runner unavailable, corrupt disk state, reconciliation error), the system continues silently. Operators have no indication that subagent lifecycle hooks were skipped or that restored runs were lost.

## Fix

Wire `log.warn()` into both catch blocks so errors are logged before the existing fallback behavior continues:

1. **Hook emission path**: `catch (err) { log.warn("failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run ${runId}: ..."); return false; }`
2. **Restore path**: `catch (err) { log.warn("failed to restore subagent runs from disk: ..."); }`

Both paths still return/continue as before (no behavior change beyond logging). The completion file gets its own `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry-completion")` to match the existing `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry")` in the registry file.

Production diff is +8 lines changed across 2 files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Two silent `catch` blocks in the subagent registry discard errors during hook emission and disk restore. The fix adds `log.warn()` to both paths so failures leave a diagnostic trail instead of being silently swallowed.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 1d5b5db) at `/tmp/fix-82943`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
Step 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-82943
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

Step 2. Verify both log.warn calls are in compiled production code
grep -n "failed to emit subagent_ended hook" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js
grep -n "failed to restore subagent runs" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js

Step 3. Start the patched gateway
timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway

Step 4. Run test suites for both changed files
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

**Compiled production code verification**

Both `log.warn` calls are present in the compiled subagent-registry bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "failed to emit subagent_ended hook" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js
94:log$2.warn(`failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run ${runId}: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);

$ grep -n "failed to restore subagent runs" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js
1911:log.warn(`failed to restore subagent runs from disk: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);
```

Line 94 is `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` (the hook emission path). Line 1911 is `restoreSubagentRunsOnce` (the disk restore path). Before this fix, both were `catch { }` (empty) and `catch { // ignore restore failures }` respectively.

The `log$2` prefix on line 94 is the compiled form of the new `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry-completion")`, while `log` on line 1911 is the existing `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry")`.

**Live gateway startup proof**

The patched gateway starts, loads the subagent registry with both warning-enabled catch blocks, and runs cleanly:

```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T12:39:28.862-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T12:39:28.917-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T12:39:33.527-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-21T12:39:34.421-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T12:39:34.422-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 5.5s)
2026-05-21T12:39:34.913-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T12:39:52.803-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-21T12:39:52.807-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T12:39:52.856-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 38ms
```

No `[agents/subagent-registry-completion]` or `[agents/subagent-registry]` warnings appear in the startup log, confirming the catch blocks are not triggered during normal operation (they are defensive logging for error paths only).

**Vitest test suites (supplemental)**

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| subagent-registry-completion.test.ts (8 tests) 86ms
 ✓ |agents-support| subagent-registry-completion.test.ts (8 tests) 73ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  16 passed (16)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| subagent-registry.test.ts (29 tests) 2679ms
 ✓ |agents-support| subagent-registry.test.ts (29 tests) 2264ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  58 passed (58)
```

**Fault injection proof: restore catch path fires in real gateway**

The `restoreSubagentRunsOnce` catch handler was triggered in a **live running gateway** by injecting `throw new Error("INJECTED: corrupt registry state")` at the top of the try block in the compiled `dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js`. This forces the catch path to fire during gateway startup.

**Steps:**
1. Back up the compiled subagent-registry bundle.
2. Inject `throw new Error(...)` inside the try block of `restoreSubagentRunsOnce` (after `restoreAttempted = true`, before `restoreSubagentRunsFromDisk`).
3. Start the gateway.
4. Observe the `log.warn` message in the gateway output.
5. Confirm the gateway continues to start normally (the catch swallows the error).
6. Restore the original bundle.

**Terminal output (fault injection):**

```console
2026-05-21T16:15:54.095-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T16:15:54.710-07:00 [agents/subagent-registry] failed to restore subagent runs from disk: INJECTED: corrupt registry state
2026-05-21T16:15:54.832-07:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
2026-05-21T16:15:56.662-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T16:15:56.665-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 2.6s)
2026-05-21T16:15:57.476-07:00 [gateway] ready
```

**Line-by-line analysis:**

- `[gateway] starting...`: Gateway startup begins.
- `[agents/subagent-registry] failed to restore subagent runs from disk: INJECTED: corrupt registry state`: The **`log.warn` in the catch handler fired**. The error message includes the full error text. Before this fix, this catch block was `catch { // ignore restore failures }` with no logging at all.
- `[gateway] ready`: The gateway **continued to start normally** despite the restore failure. The catch handler swallowed the error and let startup proceed.

Without this fix, the same error would be silently ignored (empty catch block with only a comment), leaving no diagnostic trail.

**Fault injection proof: hook emission catch path fires in real gateway**

The `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` catch handler was also triggered in a **live running gateway**. A conditional `throw` was injected inside the try block of `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` in `dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js` (line 73), and a timed trigger called the function after the gateway was ready.

**Terminal output (hook emission catch):**

```console
2026-05-21T21:35:44.608-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T21:35:58.683-07:00 [agents/subagent-registry-completion] failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run INJECTED-PROOF-RUN: INJECTED: hook runner unavailable
2026-05-21T21:35:58.686-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-21T21:35:58.734-07:00 [gmail-watcher] gmail watcher stopped
2026-05-21T21:35:58.736-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 38ms
```

**Analysis:**
- `[agents/subagent-registry-completion]` subsystem prefix confirms the **new** `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry-completion")` is active (this logger was added by this PR).
- `failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run INJECTED-PROOF-RUN: INJECTED: hook runner unavailable` is the exact `log.warn` message from the catch block at compiled line 94.
- The gateway continued running after the hook emission failure (the catch returns `false`, not rethrow).
- Before this fix, this catch block was `catch { return false; }` with no logging at all.

**Both catch blocks are now proven in live gateway runs:**
1. **Restore catch** (`restoreSubagentRunsOnce`): `[agents/subagent-registry] failed to restore subagent runs from disk: INJECTED: corrupt registry state`
2. **Hook emission catch** (`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`): `[agents/subagent-registry-completion] failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run INJECTED-PROOF-RUN: INJECTED: hook runner unavailable`

Observed result after fix: Both new `log.warn` calls fire correctly in production compiled code. The restore catch logs under `[agents/subagent-registry]` and the hook emission catch logs under `[agents/subagent-registry-completion]`. Both allow the gateway to continue running. All 74 tests pass across both test files.

What was not tested: The hook emission was triggered via injected timer call rather than a real subagent completion event. The injection proves the catch handler mechanism (log.warn + return false + gateway continues) works identically to the restore path proof.

target.title: fix(agents): match runtime policy entries when session provider is empty
target.body: ## Summary - Session entries persisted with a bare model id (no `/` prefix) silently fell through `agents.defaults.models["/"].agentRuntime` policy lookup. `modelEntryMatchKind` in `src/agents/model-runtime-policy.ts` evaluated `normalizeProviderId("anthropic") !== normalizeProviderId("")` → returned `"none"` for every provider-prefixed entry, so a configured `agentRuntime: claude-cli` was never matched. - Consequence on a production gateway running Claude subscription OAuth via `anthropic:claude-cli`: requests fell through to the embedded Anthropic-Messages harness, which sent the OAuth token (`sk-ant-oat01-…`) to `/v1/messages` and got back 404 `model_not_found`. All anthropic auth profiles cascaded into 40-min cooldown; every subsequent request surfaced `All models failed (3): … No available auth profile for anthropic` until cooldown expired. Concurrent cron/heartbeat runs that constructed a fresh runtime context with the qualified ref kept succeeding through `provider=claude-cli`. - Fix: treat empty/undefined caller provider as "no constraint" — the entry's slash-prefix is itself authoritative. Mirror in `modelKeyIsProviderWildcard`. When the caller provider is empty and multiple distinct providers would match the same bare model id, refuse to silently pick — return `{}` instead of relying on `Object.entries` order. Then short-circuit `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider` (`src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`) on known CLI aliases when caller provider is empty, because the `${provider}:${runtime}` Map lookup can't hit with empty provider. Refs `agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` sessions `d7034f5d-ee47-4c1f-95eb-e10c5793c8df` and `b74095ee-b89b-4411-a991-46da76cd84cd`. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: session entries that persisted bare model ids (no `/` prefix) silently fell through `agentRuntime: claude-cli` policy and routed Slack DMs to the embedded Anthropic-Messages harness, where Claude subscription OAuth tokens 404 against `/v1/messages` and cascade all anthropic auth profiles into cooldown. Real environment tested: Production OpenClaw 2026.5.20 gateway running on Proxmox LXC CT 112 (Debian, Node 24.13.0). Carries 8 active agents (main/Dobby, gabe, gabe-voice, researcher, forge, architect, marcus, scribe), 27 cron jobs, live Slack channel (`agent:main:slack:direct:u01q3fya3s4`), `anthropic:claude-cli` OAuth subscription auth (Claude Pro Max). Bundle-level equivalent of this patch applied to the installed `dist/model-runtime-policy-pAmabiPO.js` + `dist/model-runtime-aliases-CF-sDqQj.js`, gateway restarted, real Slack DM sent. Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Applied the equivalent patch to the two installed bundle files, verified with `node -c`. 2. `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway.service`. 3. Sent a real Slack DM to the `main` agent from Brian's user account through the existing `agent:main:slack:direct:u01q3fya3s4` channel: "Confirm your runtime: which provider+model is serving this reply, and was your auth profile in cooldown?" 4. Tailed `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-24.log` for matching events. 5. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.test.ts` — 28 tests pass. Evidence after fix: Gateway log lines triggered by the Slack DM (timestamps CDT): ``` 02:30:50.766 cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=claude-opus-4-7[1m] promptChars=834 trigger=user useResume=true session=present resumeSession=27c5793ef4a7 reuse=reusable 02:31:04.215 claude live session turn: provider=claude-cli model=claude-opus-4-7[1m] durationMs=13431 rawLines=44 ``` Dobby's self-reported reply over Slack: > "Runtime: claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7[1m] via anthropic:claude-cli (OAuth, Claude Pro Max). Not in cooldown — auth healthy, fallbacks armed (opus-4-7, opus-4-6). Gateway came up 9m ago on 2026.5.20 (e510042)." Test suite outputs (paired with the real-environment evidence above, not standalone): - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.test.ts` — `Test Files 3 passed (3), Tests 28 passed (28)`. - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ` — `exit=0`. - `pnpm tsgo:core` — `exit=0`. - `pnpm check:changed` — typecheck core green; pre-existing `src/plugins/document-extractors.runtime.test.ts(53,74): error TS2554` on clean `origin/main e9ca3115f0` and unrelated to this patch (verified by stash/un-stash). Observed result after fix: same Slack DM lane that was 404-cascading and cooldown-spinning yesterday now routes through the `claude-cli` subprocess. `provider=claude-cli`, `durationMs=13.4s`, 44 raw output lines, no `embedded run` events, no `errorPreview`, no `auth_profile_failure_state_updated`, no `lane task error`. Concurrent cron jobs continue to succeed in parallel as they did before. What was not tested: `google-gemini-cli` equivalent path — no failing session existed to replay, and the fix is structurally symmetric with `claude-cli` so the same defensive logic applies. Doctor's `codex-route-warnings.ts` callers — they always pass a qualified ref via `parseModelRef`, never empty, so the empty-provider arm of the patch never fires for them. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix: resolve response prefix template variables in abort replies
target.body:
## Summary

Template variables are not populated when user issues early stop commands. 

- **Problem:** When a user sends a "stop" command (e.g. on WhatsApp), the abort reply displays unresolved template variables: `{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`
- **Why it matters:** The raw `{model}` and `{thinkingLevel}` placeholders leak into user-facing messages, looking broken and confusing.
- **What changed:** `createReplyPrefixContext()` now pre-seeds the `ResponsePrefixContext` with model, provider, and thinkingLevel resolved from config defaults, so early-exit paths like abort have valid values for template interpolation.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to abort logic, dispatch flow, template resolution engine, or `formatAbortReplyText()`. The `onModelSelected()` callback still overwrites the defaults at runtime during normal replies.

## Change Type (select all)

- [X] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [X] Gateway / orchestration
- [X] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #45314

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Abort replies now display the resolved model name and thinking level from config (e.g. `kimi-k2.5 - off 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`) instead of literal `{model} - {thinkingLevel}` placeholders.
- Applies to all channels using a `responsePrefix` template with `{model}` or `{thinkingLevel}` variables.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / pnpm
- Model/provider: openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5
- Integration/channel: WhatsApp (reproduces on any channel with `responsePrefix` template)
- Relevant config:
  ```json
  {
    "messages": {
      "responsePrefix": "{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: "
    }
  }
  ```

### Steps

1. Configure a `responsePrefix` with `{model}` and/or `{thinkingLevel}` template variables
2. Start a conversation so the agent is processing
3. Send "stop" or `/stop`

### Expected

- Reply: `kimi-k2.5 - off 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`

### Actual

- Reply: `{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`

## Evidence
Before
![before](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73faefe5-65c3-488d-a429-94edf51ca959)
After
![after](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e70f899-9353-4679-a63a-4272a8d7d197)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `/stop` command triggers abort reply with resolved prefix; normal replies still resolve correctly after model selection overwrites defaults
- Edge cases checked: No model configured (falls back to hardcoded defaults); `onModelSelected` correctly overwrites pre-seeded values at runtime

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit
- Files/config to restore: `src/channels/reply-prefix.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Incorrect model name or thinking level in reply prefix (would indicate config resolution mismatch)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `resolveDefaultModelForAgent()` or `resolveThinkingDefault()` could add overhead to context creation.
  - Mitigation: Both are lightweight synchronous config lookups with no I/O; already used elsewhere in the hot path.

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/channels/reply-prefix.ts` | Pre-seed `ResponsePrefixContext` with default model/provider/thinkingLevel from config |
| `src/channels/reply-prefix.test.ts` | New test: verifies pre-seeded context, `onModelSelected` override, and fallback defaults |


---

## Notes

- This PR is AI-assisted (fully tested: `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` — 919 test files, 2642 tests passing).
- Add before/after screenshots showing the abort reply with unresolved vs resolved template variables.
- The root cause is that the abort fast-path in `dispatch-from-config.ts` exits before the agent run triggers `onModelSelected()`, so the prefix context never got model/thinkingLevel populated. The fix is entirely in context initialization — no changes to abort or dispatch logic.

target.title: fix(secrets): generate secretref reference docs from the credential matrix
target.body:
## Summary

Generates the SecretRef credential reference docs (`docs/reference/secretref-user-supplied-credentials-matrix.json` and the marker-block lists in `docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md`) from the same registry-backed source that already powers `src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts`, so contributor branches stop hitting CI drift on artifacts that should never need hand-editing.

Closes #44289.

- **Single source of truth**: both reference artifacts are now derived from `buildSecretRefCredentialMatrix()`. Updating `src/secrets/target-registry-data.ts` (or any bundled-channel unsupported pattern) no longer requires editing two separate docs files.
- **Generator + check pair**: `scripts/generate-secretref-credential-matrix.ts` now accepts `--check`. Without flags it rewrites the JSON matrix and the supported/unsupported marker blocks in the Markdown reference; with `--check` it exits non-zero with a regenerate hint when either artifact is out of sync.
- **package.json scripts**: `pnpm gen:secretref-docs` (writer) and `pnpm check:secretref-docs` (verifier).
- **Preflight wiring**: `scripts/check.mjs` runs `check:secretref-docs` in the preflight stage so contributor branches catch drift before typecheck/lint, not at the tail of CI.
- **Drift test stays in sync with the writer**: `target-registry.docs.test.ts` now compares on-disk artifacts against the same formatter the script uses, so the test cannot diverge from the writer. The existing registry-shape parity check is preserved as a third independent guard.
- **Formatter unit tests** (`credential-matrix-docs.test.ts`): 10 isolated tests cover ordering, sibling_ref rendering (`channels.googlechat.serviceAccount` compatibility exception), the `auth-profiles.json` subsection annotation, empty-list handling, idempotence, and the missing-marker error paths.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Test

## Scope

- [x] Docs / generated reference
- [x] Tooling / build scripts

## Linked Issue

- Closes #44289

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: The two SecretRef reference artifacts were hand-maintained while only `src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts` caught divergence. Editing `src/secrets/target-registry-data.ts` (or adding a bundled-channel unsupported pattern) silently produced reference files that did not match the registry until a later CI failure on an unrelated branch. ClawSweeper triaged this in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/44289#issuecomment-4523949220 and labelled it `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`.

**Real environment tested**: Local macOS 15.7.4 (Darwin 25.4.0 arm64) host running Node v22.22.0 and Corepack-managed pnpm against the branch HEAD `9b8cc969`. No remote services were involved; the change is purely doc generation, package scripts, preflight wiring, and tests. The new generator and check script were run end-to-end against the real working tree, including a destructive drift simulation and a byte-for-byte restore via `gen:secretref-docs`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Five real terminal sessions were run end-to-end against the patched working tree. (1) `pnpm gen:secretref-docs` to confirm idempotence on a clean tree. (2) `pnpm check:secretref-docs` to confirm exit 0 on the generator output. (3) Destructive drift simulation: `cp docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md /tmp/orig.md && sed -i '' '/channels.discord.token/d' docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md && pnpm check:secretref-docs && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts` to verify the check fails closed and the drift test surfaces the same divergence. (4) Regeneration restore: `pnpm gen:secretref-docs && pnpm check:secretref-docs && diff /tmp/orig.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md` to verify byte-for-byte recovery. (5) Full repo guards on the touched paths: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/secrets/credential-matrix-docs.test.ts src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts src/secrets/target-registry.test.ts && pnpm format:check <touched files> && pnpm lint <touched files>`.

**Evidence after fix**: Redacted real terminal output from the local working tree, captured at branch HEAD before push.

Generator idempotence on a clean tree:

```
$ pnpm gen:secretref-docs
> OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions ... tsx scripts/generate-secretref-credential-matrix.ts
Wrote docs/reference/secretref-user-supplied-credentials-matrix.json
docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md already in sync
$ echo $?
0
```

Check mode passes on the generated tree:

```
$ pnpm check:secretref-docs
> OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions ... tsx scripts/generate-secretref-credential-matrix.ts --check
secretref reference docs are in sync.
$ echo $?
0
```

Destructive drift simulation: removing one supported entry from the docs causes both the check script and the drift test to fail closed:

```
$ sed -i '' '/channels.discord.token/d' docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md
$ pnpm check:secretref-docs
error: docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md marker blocks are out of sync.
Run `pnpm gen:secretref-docs` to regenerate.
[ELIFECYCLE] Command failed with exit code 1.
$ echo $?
1
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts
 FAIL  src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts > credential-surface marker blocks match generator output
 FAIL  src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts > matrix and credential-surface marker blocks describe the same registry shape
Test Files  1 failed (1)
     Tests  2 failed | 1 passed (3)
```

Regeneration restores the tree byte-for-byte:

```
$ pnpm gen:secretref-docs
Wrote docs/reference/secretref-user-supplied-credentials-matrix.json
Updated marker blocks in docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md
$ pnpm check:secretref-docs
secretref reference docs are in sync.
$ diff /tmp/orig.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md
$ echo $?
0
```

Full guards on the touched paths after fix:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    src/secrets/credential-matrix-docs.test.ts \
    src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts \
    src/secrets/target-registry.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7
 PASS  src/secrets/credential-matrix-docs.test.ts (10 tests) 19ms
 PASS  src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts (3 tests) 649ms
 PASS  src/secrets/target-registry.test.ts (5 tests) 174ms
Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  18 passed (18)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json
$ echo $?
0

$ pnpm lint <touched files>
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ pnpm format:check <touched files>
All matched files use the correct format.
```

**Observed result after fix**: A contributor editing `src/secrets/target-registry-data.ts` runs `pnpm gen:secretref-docs` once and both reference artifacts update in lockstep. The new preflight `check:secretref-docs` stage of `scripts/check.mjs` surfaces drift in the preflight guards alongside `dup:check:coverage`, before the typecheck or lint stages, so contributor branches no longer hit late CI drift failures on the SecretRef reference docs. The destructive simulation captured above confirms the check fails closed (exit 1) and the regenerator restores the working tree byte-for-byte (`diff` exit 0).

**What was not tested**: This patch does not touch runtime SecretRef resolution or audit behaviour, so the existing live SecretRef resolution coverage in `target-registry.test.ts` is unchanged. No CI workflow changes were exercised end-to-end beyond the preflight wiring already covered by `scripts/check.mjs` in local runs; the actual CI run on this PR exercises the new preflight stage in the real GitHub Actions environment.

## Root Cause

- `scripts/generate-secretref-credential-matrix.ts` only wrote the JSON matrix; the Markdown reference's two marker blocks were edited by hand even though their content is fully determined by the registry.
- `src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts` detected drift but provided no path to regenerate, so contributors had to mirror the JSON change into the Markdown by hand and hope the test agreed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test (new formatter tests in `credential-matrix-docs.test.ts`)
  - [x] Integration / drift test (rewritten `target-registry.docs.test.ts` now mirrors the writer)
  - [x] CI preflight (new `check:secretref-docs` runs alongside `dup:check:coverage`)
- **Target test files**:
  - `src/secrets/credential-matrix-docs.test.ts` (new — formatter contract)
  - `src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts` (rewritten — generator parity)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- No runtime behavior change. SecretRef eligibility, resolution, and audit coverage are unchanged.
- `docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md` supported-list entries are now deterministically ordered by id within the `openclaw.json` subsection (instead of registry-walk order). Marker comments, surrounding prose, and the `auth-profiles.json` subsection heading are unchanged. The unsupported list preserves the registry-walk order (`CORE_UNSUPPORTED_SECRETREF_SURFACE_PATTERNS` followed by bundled-channel patterns), matching the existing `getUnsupportedSecretRefSurfacePatterns()` contract.

## Scope boundary

- Only the generator, two reference docs, the drift test, two new tooling scripts, the preflight wiring, the knip allowlist entry, and the CHANGELOG `Unreleased` entry are touched.
- No changes to runtime SecretRef resolution, no new runtime dependencies, no install or CI workflow changes beyond the preflight inclusion.

target.title: fix(ui): scope chat session picker to active agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Control UI chat session picker can show sessions from other configured agents because picker search/load-more calls are not scoped by active agent and the popover renders raw `sessions.list` rows.
- Why it matters: multi-agent users can see and accidentally switch into unrelated agent/service sessions from the chat picker.
- What changed: picker requests now include the active `agentId`, and the popover renders rows through the same filtered session-option projection used by the grouped selector.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the full Sessions page and gateway `sessions.list` behavior remain unchanged.

AI-assisted: Yes. I understand the code path and verified the targeted behavior locally.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85963
- Supersedes #85964 after rebasing onto current `main`
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: chat session picker pagination/search built `sessions.list` params without the active `agentId`, then rendered `result.sessions` directly instead of using the existing filtered session option groups.
- Missing detection / guardrail: picker tests covered search/pagination mechanics but not cross-agent/cron/subagent filtering in the popover.
- Contributing context (if known): the grouped selector already had active-agent filtering, but the newer popover path bypassed it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when the picker result includes another agent, cron session, and spawned subagent, only active-agent chat sessions render in the popover.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the Control UI projection/render path, so a focused DOM render test covers the visible regression without a browser E2E fixture.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): grouped selector filtering tests in `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` covered the helper but not this popover path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The chat session picker now shows active-agent chat sessions by default instead of leaking unrelated agent/service rows into the popover.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[open chat picker] -> [sessions.list without agentId] -> [raw rows rendered] -> [cross-agent rows visible]

After:
[open chat picker] -> [sessions.list with active agentId] -> [filtered option rows rendered] -> [active-agent sessions visible]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: UI session-list access is narrowed for this picker by passing `agentId` and filtering display rows; broader session management surfaces are unchanged.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local pnpm workspace and installed local OpenClaw Control UI
- Model/provider: N/A for test execution
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI chat
- Relevant config (redacted): multiple configured agents with agent-scoped session keys

### Steps

1. Open the Control UI chat view with multiple configured agents and sessions.
2. Open the chat session picker.
3. Search/load more from the picker.

### Expected

- Picker requests are scoped to the active agent.
- Popover renders only sessions allowed by the grouped session selector filtering.

### Actual

- Before this PR, picker requests were unscoped and the popover rendered raw `sessions.list` rows.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Redacted runtime diagnostic output
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 11.10s

pnpm check:test-types
completed successfully

pnpm build
completed successfully
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: the Control UI chat session picker can leak unrelated configured-agent/service rows when it requests sessions without the active agent scope and renders raw response rows.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw app, Control UI served from the installed app on `127.0.0.1:18789`, with multiple configured agents present. Private session labels, keys, and structure are intentionally redacted from the proof below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: refreshed the installed Control UI cache, inspected the served HTML/JS/SW, and queried the live gateway with an explicit active-agent filter. No screenshots are attached because the visible picker contains private session names.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:18789/ | rg -n 'index-BtIuF4zW\.js\?v=local-session-picker-fix|local-session-picker-fix'
198:    <script type="module" crossorigin src="./assets/index-BtIuF4zW.js?v=local-session-picker-fix"></script>

$ curl -sS 'http://127.0.0.1:18789/assets/index-BtIuF4zW.js?v=local-session-picker-fix' \
  | rg -o 'agentId:EW\(e,e\.sessionKey\)|i=AW\(e,e\.sessionKey,r\)\.flatMap|local-session-picker-fix'
agentId:EW(e,e.sessionKey)
i=AW(e,e.sessionKey,r).flatMap
local-session-picker-fix

$ curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:18789/sw.js \
  | rg -n 'local-session-picker-fix|cacheName: CACHE_NAME|caches\.match'
5:const EMBEDDED_CACHE_VERSION = "2026.5.22-a374c3a5bfd5-local-session-picker-fix";
64:      caches.match(event.request, { cacheName: CACHE_NAME }).then(
86:        .catch(() => caches.match(event.request)),

$ openclaw gateway call sessions.list --json --params '{"agentId":"main","limit":50}' \
  | jq '{agentScopedCount:(.sessions|length), containsForeignAgents:([.sessions[] | select((.agentId // "main") != "main")] | length > 0), visibleRowsRedacted:[.sessions[:5][] | {key:"<redacted>", label:"<redacted>", agentId:(.agentId // "main"), kind:(.kind // null)}]}'
{
  "agentScopedCount": 22,
  "containsForeignAgents": false,
  "visibleRowsRedacted": [
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "group" },
    { "key": "<redacted>", "label": "<redacted>", "agentId": "main", "kind": "direct" }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the served picker bundle now sends the active `agentId`, projects picker rows through the existing filtered option groups, and the live gateway diagnostic for the active agent reports `containsForeignAgents: false` with session keys/labels redacted.
- What was not tested: public screenshot/recording of the picker, because that would reveal private session names and structure. The runtime diagnostic above is the privacy-preserving proof artifact.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: picker search params include `agentId`; popover filters out other-agent, cron, and spawned subagent rows; targeted UI tests pass; test typecheck passes; full build passes; installed local dist contains the scoped picker request and filtered-row projection.
- Edge cases checked: active session remains visible through existing `resolveSessionOptionGroups` behavior; pagination/search test expectations were updated for the scoped request.
- What you did **not** verify: public browser screenshots in a running Control UI instance, because they would expose private session names and structure.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the picker count can reflect the filtered visible rows while `totalCount` still comes from the server response.
  - Mitigation: server request is now scoped by `agentId`, and existing filtered option logic is reused for display consistency.

target.title: fix(auth): explain masked oauth profiles
target.body:
## Summary

- Normalize legacy `openai-codex` OAuth auth-profile aliases on load so older stored profiles remain usable.
- Add a targeted diagnostic when a stored auth profile exists for a provider but is skipped because `auth.profiles.<id>.mode` does not match the stored credential type.
- This now covers both #47964 failure modes: alias-shaped OAuth credentials and stale `mode=api_key` masking a valid OAuth profile.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #47964
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

There were two adjacent present-but-unusable OpenAI Codex OAuth paths:

- Older `auth-profiles.json` entries could contain legacy OAuth aliases such as `access_token`, `refreshToken`, `expires_at`, or `account_id`. The loader preserved those fields, but downstream PI credential conversion only reads canonical `access`, `refresh`, and finite `expires`, so the profile was ignored.
- A stale config override could set `auth.profiles.<id>.mode` to `api_key` while the stored credential was OAuth. Auth profile ordering skipped the stored OAuth credential, then the final error only reported a missing API key.

## What Changed

- Normalize known legacy OpenAI Codex OAuth aliases to canonical fields during persisted profile parsing.
- Keep canonical stored fields authoritative if both canonical and alias fields exist.
- Detect config/store auth-profile mode mismatches before throwing the final missing-auth error.
- Include a secret-free hint naming only profile/provider/mode metadata.
- Add regression coverage for both the legacy alias profile and the stale `api_key` mode conflict.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Only compatibility normalization on read for known OpenAI Codex OAuth aliases
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

Diagnostics deliberately report only profile/provider/mode metadata. Regression coverage asserts OAuth access and refresh token values are not included in the diagnostic.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Verified the auth-profile surface after this patch still exposes a real OpenAI Codex OAuth profile as an OAuth profile instead of masking it as missing API-key auth.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw install with a running LaunchAgent gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`; real user auth-profile store under `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent`.

target.title: fix(auth): add --token flag to paste-token and surface validTypes in invalid_type warning
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #63042. Improves non-interactive auth setup and debuggability of auth profile load errors.

## Changes

- Adds `openclaw models auth paste-token --token <value>` for trusted scripted setup.
- Keeps the existing interactive `paste-token` prompt when `--token` is omitted.
- Rejects empty `--token` values before persisting credentials.
- Validates Anthropic setup-token values before persisting them.
- Includes accepted `validTypes` in invalid auth-profile warning logs.
- Documents `--token` and its argv exposure tradeoff in `docs/cli/models.md`.
- Adds an Unreleased changelog entry for the user-facing CLI fix.

## Security note

`--token <value>` is a convenience path for trusted shells and automation, but inline secrets can be exposed through shell history, command echoing, CI logs, or process-list tools. The CLI help and docs now say this explicitly and recommend the interactive prompt when possible.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `models auth paste-token --token <value>` now runs through the real CLI without opening the interactive token prompt, updates the auth profile, and prints the normal config/profile update output.

Real environment tested: Local checkout of PR head `57874b9b64034f7b49719067b03f29683c909b33` on 2026-05-08, running the real OpenClaw CLI through `corepack pnpm openclaw` with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  corepack pnpm openclaw models auth paste-token \
    --provider openai \
    --profile-id proof:manual \
    --token proof-redacted-token
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the real CLI command above:

```console
Updated $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Auth profile: proof:manual (openai/token)
```

Observed result after fix: The command completed successfully without an interactive prompt and wrote the requested `proof:manual` OpenAI token auth profile in the isolated OpenClaw home.

What was not tested: A real provider token was not used; the changed behavior under test is CLI non-interactive token ingestion and profile persistence, not provider-side authentication.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/models-cli.ts src/commands/models/auth.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted.ts src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts docs/cli/models.md CHANGELOG.md`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] Real CLI proof above shows `models auth paste-token --token` completes without an interactive prompt.

target.title: fix(imessage): detect and recover anchorless watch payloads before routing
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix iMessage group link-preview watch payloads that lose their conversation anchor (`chat_id=0`, empty `chat_guid` / `chat_identifier`, `is_group=false`) before they can be classified as sender DMs.
- Move anchorless recovery/fail-closed drop ahead of live inbound debounce classification so `coalesceSameSenderDms` cannot merge malformed group payloads under the raw `chat:0` DM bucket.
- Keep the existing recovery path for catchup/direct handling and add monitor-level regression coverage for the live coalescing path.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: iMessage group link-preview watch payloads can arrive without usable conversation anchor fields. Before this fix, live notifications entered the inbound debouncer first, so with `coalesceSameSenderDms=true` multiple anchorless group payloads from the same sender could be merged under `imessage:<account>:dm:chat:0:<sender>` before recovery. The merged turn was then repaired using only the first GUID and could route content from another group under the first recovered group.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0, OpenClaw source checkout at `e22876fd1befdbda3183cc33fb5162459791a89e`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483/node_modules/.bin/vitest run \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor.watch-subscribe-retry.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor.gating.test.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts

pnpm -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 check:test-types

env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483/node_modules/.bin/tsx \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84503-anchorless-proof.mts

OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19046 \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84503-final-runtime/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84503-final-runtime/state/openclaw.json \
node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway

curl -sS -I http://127.0.0.1:19046/
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       51 passed (51)
Duration    9.29s
```

```text
$ pnpm -C /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-41483 check:test-types
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

```text
=== PR #84503 anchorless iMessage proof ===
PASS single anchorless payload recovers to group chat_id=101
PASS unrecoverable anchorless payload is dropped fail-closed
BEFORE fix: raw payloads share imessage:default:dm:chat:0:+15550001111; merged GUIDs=["GUID-GROUP-A","GUID-GROUP-B"] route=chat_id:101
AFTER fix: payloads repair before debounce; keys=imessage:default:chat:101:+15550001111 / imessage:default:chat:202:+15550001111; routes=chat_id:101,chat_id:202
PASS coalescing risk closed: cross-chat anchorless payloads no longer enter one raw DM bucket
Overall: PASS
```

```text
2026-05-24T15:58:29.888+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 9.6s)
2026-05-24T15:58:30.678+08:00 [gateway] ready
```

```text
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
```

**Observed result after fix**: Anchorless live payloads are repaired before entering the inbound debouncer. Recovered group payloads have `is_group=true` and distinct group chat IDs before debounce classification, so `coalesceSameSenderDms` no longer places different malformed groups into the same raw `chat:0` DM bucket. Single anchorless payload recovery and unrecoverable fail-closed drops still work.

**What was not tested**: A real BlueBubbles/iMessage live watch feed was not connected in this Linux environment. The gateway startup used `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, so it proves the patched OpenClaw runtime starts and serves HTTP, but it does not exercise a live macOS Messages database or live BlueBubbles transport. The iMessage behavior proof uses the real monitor/conversation-repair/coalesce TypeScript modules with redacted synthetic message IDs and handles.

Fixes #84470

target.title: refactor(terminal): optimize OSC progress label sanitization
target.body:
### Summary

Replaced the OSC progress label sanitizer's chained string passes with one module-level precompiled regex. The forbidden label tokens are still the same: OSC ST, BEL, C1 ST, bare ESC, and `]`.

### Benchmark

Environment: Node `v22.17.0` on native Windows, run from `C:\Users\user\Downloads\openclaw-pr`.

Method:
- compared the previous chained `replaceAll` / `split().join()` sanitizer against the new regex sanitizer
- verified old and new outputs matched for every benchmark input before timing
- used warmup plus 7 timing rounds per case and reported the median

Results:
- `plain-label` (26 chars): old `576.3 ns/op`, new `88.2 ns/op` -> `6.53x` faster (`-84.7%`)
- `short-osc` (11 chars): old `863.8 ns/op`, new `181.2 ns/op` -> `4.77x` faster (`-79.0%`)
- `mixed-controls` (20 chars): old `1274.2 ns/op`, new `333.5 ns/op` -> `3.82x` faster (`-73.8%`)
- `long-status` (2559 chars): old `23663.9 ns/op`, new `21921.4 ns/op` -> `1.08x` faster (`-7.4%`)

### Tests

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` completed. Note: emitted the existing engine warning because this local machine has Node `v22.17.0` while the repo asks for `>=22.19.0`.
- `pnpm test src/terminal/osc-progress.test.ts` passed: 1 test file, 2 tests.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/terminal/osc-progress.ts src/terminal/osc-progress.test.ts CHANGELOG.md` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

### Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OSC progress labels are sanitized before being interpolated into terminal OSC 9;4 progress sequences.

Real environment tested: Native Windows checkout at `C:\Users\user\Downloads\openclaw-pr`, Node `v22.17.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the patched checkout, ran a local inline `node` benchmark script that invokes the old sanitizer and new sanitizer on representative OSC progress labels, asserts output equality before timing, then reports median nanoseconds per operation.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output copied from the patched checkout:

```text
$ node <inline osc-progress sanitizer benchmark script>
plain-label     length=  26 old=576.3 ns/op new=88.2 ns/op speedup=6.53x reduction=84.7%
short-osc       length=  11 old=863.8 ns/op new=181.2 ns/op speedup=4.77x reduction=79.0%
mixed-controls  length=  20 old=1274.2 ns/op new=333.5 ns/op speedup=3.82x reduction=73.8%
long-status     length=2559 old=23663.9 ns/op new=21921.4 ns/op speedup=1.08x reduction=7.4%
```

Observed result after fix: The benchmark completed after checking old/new output equality for plain, OSC-containing, mixed-control, and long repeated labels. The new sanitizer preserved the expected sanitized labels, including `Buildsafebaddone` for mixed OSC terminators/control characters, and measured faster across all benchmark cases.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Ghostty/WezTerm terminal session or the full repository test suite.

target.title: fix(docker): create /home/node/.config with node:node ownership
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes EACCES errors when npx (or other tools) needs to write to `~/.config` in Docker slim images.

## Problem

The `install -d /home/node/.config/openclaw` command auto-creates the parent directory `/home/node/.config` as `root:root` when run as root. This causes EACCES errors for the `node` user when tools like `npx` try to write to `~/.config`.

## Fix

Explicitly create `/home/node/.config` with `-o node -g node` ownership, and add a stat assertion to verify.

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `Dockerfile` | +2 | Add `/home/node/.config` to install -d + stat assertion |

Fixes: #85968
